title: Asuka in The Military
wikitext:
[]: Asuka in The Military             Quote Post by ChaddyManPrime » Tue Jun 03, 2014 2:58 am How did Asuka being in the Military help improve her? Did it improve her better than College did? Can you see improvements if there are indeed any? What would the Military improve about her? I honestly don't know jack about the Military, what are the benefits of it and can you see them in her? I'm thinking along the lines of what the military has to offer Asuka in terms of improving her emotional stability. How did it improve Shikinami over Soryu? Do you see improvement in Shikinami? What does being in Military bestow upon people? Compared to Soryu, what do you see in Shikinami that could be attributed to the military and can be seen as an improvement towards her attitude in life?

[]:             Quote Post by ElMariachi » Tue Jun 03, 2014 3:24 am ^ She IS in the military in Rebuild, Misato says in 2.0 that she has the rank of Captain of the European Air Force, whatever that means (Captains in the Air Forces in general command a squadron, Asuka never showed any leadership skills until 3.0) And in 3.0 WILLE is military in everything but name.

[]:             Quote Post by ChaddyManPrime » Tue Jun 03, 2014 3:25 am ^ I accidentally asked it wrong, hold on Good Sir. Edit: Hopefully I explained what I am asking better

[]:             Quote Post by ElMariachi » Tue Jun 03, 2014 3:42 am ^ Well then, it's very difficult (even nigh impossible) to know how much being in the military improved or worsened her, since Shikinami has a different base personality than Soryu(we're not even sure about the former's background with her mother being the same than her NGE counterpart for starters)

[]:             Quote Post by ChaddyManPrime » Tue Jun 03, 2014 3:48 am ^ Well what does being in Military bestow upon people? Compared to Soryu, what do you see in Shikinami that could be attributed to the military and can be seen as an improvement? This question goes to everybody.

[]:             Quote Post by Rosenakahara » Tue Jun 03, 2014 4:07 am well the 14 year gap must have improved her a little (now whether or not that has to do with her inclusion in a military unit remains to be seen), while she wouldnt have a fit over it like sohryu would, shikinami would still be pissed that Shinji beat her, whereas in 3.0 it was more mild annoyance, hell she was the one who went out of her way to find Shinji after the battle so that shows she has matured at least

[]:             Quote Post by Kendrix » Tue Jun 03, 2014 6:46 am I think her Pre-timeskip rank was more of the honorary sort, perhaps given to her to keep her satisfied, or because she's technically (as an EVA pilot) part of the European Airforce. After timeskip is of course a very different matter, she's all no-nonsense professional amazon queen now, no more pre-battle makeup... She was stuck in a hellish world, and well, it was adapt or die. It was probably a painful, breaking process, hence why she's so bitter and pissed-off now.

[]:             Quote Post by SoryuUberAlles » Tue Jun 03, 2014 9:35 am Whenever I watched M*A*S*H I always asked why people like Hawkeye were given such senior ranks. Apparently, people who were drafted with postgraduate degrees were automatically offered Commission Officer ranks. So maybe it has something to do with her unusually precocious degree. Kendrix wrote: I think her Pre-timeskip rank was more of the honorary sort, perhaps given to her to keep her satisfied, or because she's technically (as an EVA pilot) part of the European Airforce

[]:             Quote Post by AR-99 » Tue Jun 03, 2014 12:02 pm ^ Captain would be a mid level rank. Junior officers would be the grades of lieutenant below it. Since my mom watches M*A*S*H, I get roped in too. Hawkeye, BJ, and Trapper are all captains per this: "In Army and Air Force medical units, captain is the entry-level rank for those possessing a medical degree, or a doctorate in a healthcare profession". Hawkeye gets away with a lot of crap that is right on the edge of insubordination if not outright, which is why you see Burns pulling rank on him whenever he can. Back to the original question though: ChaddyManPrime wrote: Well what does being in Military bestow upon people? Compared to Soryu, what do you see in Shikinami that could be attributed to the military and can be seen as an improvement?

[]:             Quote Post by Ray » Tue Jun 03, 2014 1:29 pm Wheres GobbHobblin? He knows more about Military rankings than the majority of us.

[]:             Quote Post by ChaddyManPrime » Tue Jun 03, 2014 2:02 pm @ AR-99 I kinda thinking more along the lines of what the military has to offer Asuka in terms of improving her emotional stability. How did it improve Shikinami over Soryu? Do you see improvement in Shikinami? Again this question is for everybody. @ Ray That is an excellent idea, we should ask the Good Sir.

[]:             Quote Post by AR-99 » Tue Jun 03, 2014 6:24 pm If we're talking emotional stability, then there would be a correlation between how she handles herself in combat or crisis. I don't count the Eva-03 infection as combat though and what she could do, if anything, was nonexistent. I see more maturity in 3.0, also factoring in the 28 y/o biological age. Shikinami's conduct under combat stress is decisive and she does not panic. The Eva-13 fight is worth noting as she goes all out.

[]:             Quote Post by Jimbob » Tue Jun 03, 2014 7:16 pm Some miltiary who are important assets are automatically jumped in rank, because protocol dictates they can't have low rankers doing it, or to lessen the amount of people they need to take orders from, or just because they've transfered in from somewhere where they don't need to prove their worth because they've already done so. This happens a lot in robot shows, typically when the pilot is someone who hasn't even been through basic training but is the only one that can pilot the robot and only officers are pilots. Asuka's rank is probably something like that. Granted she's probably well respected because Europe seems to geniunely respect and understand an Eva pilots job, and not resented, but I doubt she spent years working from a fresh recruit to Captain. She was probably promoted right upon joining.

[]:             Quote Post by BlueBasilisk » Wed Jun 04, 2014 1:18 am Kendrix wrote: I think her Pre-timeskip rank was more of the honorary sort, perhaps given to her to keep her satisfied, or because she's technically (as an EVA pilot) part of the European Airforce. After timeskip is of course a very different matter, she's all no-nonsense professional amazon queen now, no more pre-battle makeup... She was stuck in a hellish world, and well, it was adapt or die. It was probably a painful, breaking process, hence why she's so bitter and pissed-off now.

[]:             Quote Post by ElMariachi » Wed Jun 04, 2014 3:35 am AR-99 wrote: Shikinami's degree isn't mentioned

[]:             Quote Post by Rosenakahara » Wed Jun 04, 2014 4:37 am ElMariachi wrote: Actually, it was mentioned in a throwaway line by Kensuke when the Three Stooges are in the train station, so she's even more of a hot shit than in NGE. But anyway I don't think that we should take 3.0 into account when trying to determine how her military background influenced her, since there's the 14 extra years of experience that surely had a much bigger part in changing her. Even Mari, who so far doesn't have any military background, has gone from a crazy melee enthusiast wrecking every Eva she pilots to a cold-headed sniper, the polar opposite, in the span of those 14 years. As BlueBasilisk said, her military training clearly gave her an huge edge on maneuvering her Eva, as her introductory scene showed us, but I don't think that she made her that much better in terms of attitude : first she was hellbent on being the superstar of the team and wanted to go alone against Sahaquiel, even arguing repetitively with Misato, her commanding officer , you try that to your CO if you're in the army and see what happens (hint: you won't like it), and when faced with something unexpected (Sahaquiel's core being mobile) she completely froze, unable to think of what to do, and it's Rei's quick thinking of grabbing the core to give Asuka an opening that saved the day. Thankfully, instead of emotionally self-destruct like Soryu did, Shikinami draw lessons from that battles and learned that relying on others isn't a weakness, but it's impossible to know if that difference comes from her military background or simply their personality being different. (and maybe Shikinami don't have Soryu's traumatic background) On a side-note : I'm the only one that think that it was quite the contrary, that she completely botched her fight against EVA-13? I mean she let her rage take possession of her so much that she didn't paid any attention to her battery level, and ended out of energy right in front of the enemy! And said enemy wasn't even trying to fight her! Asuka was really lucky that it was EVA-13 piloted by Shinji and not Kaworu or worst, the Mark.09, or she would had ended as paste.

[]:             Quote Post by ElMariachi » Wed Jun 04, 2014 7:45 am ^ Personally I took it as a hint that she's not as mature as she paints herself to be.

[]:             Quote Post by Rosenakahara » Wed Jun 04, 2014 7:55 am ElMariachi wrote: ^ Personally I took it as a hint that she's not as mature as she paints herself to be.

[]:             Quote Post by ElMariachi » Wed Jun 04, 2014 9:24 am ^ That's how I interpreted her actions in the movie, on that we perfectly agree.

[]:             Quote Post by Kendrix » Wed Jun 04, 2014 8:40 pm Rosenakahara wrote: i don't think Asuka is really trying to be mature, she never goes out of her way to act like the better person in the situation nor try to hidee her emotions to seem "mature",

Article: Asuka in The Military
 surely had a much bigger part in changing her. Even Mari, who so far doesn't have any military background, has gone from a crazy melee enthusiast wrecking every Eva she pilots to a cold-headed sniper, the polar opposite, in the span of those 14 years. As BlueBasilisk said, her military training clearly gave her an huge edge on maneuvering her Eva, as her introductory scene showed us, but I don't think that she made her that much better in terms of attitude : first she was hellbent on being the superstar of the team and wanted to go alone against Sahaquiel, even arguing repetitively with Misato, her commanding officer , you try that to your CO if you're in the army and see what happens (hint: you won't like it), and when faced with something unexpected (Sahaquiel's core being mobile) she completely froze, unable to think of what to do, and it's Rei's quick thinking of grabbing the core to give Asuka an opening that saved the day. Thankfully, instead of emotionally self-destruct like Soryu did, Shikinami draw lessons from that battles and learned that relying on others isn't a weakness, but it's impossible to know if that difference comes from her military background or simply their personality being different. (and maybe Shikinami don't have Soryu's traumatic background) On a side-note : I'm the only one that think that it was quite the contrary, that she completely botched her fight against EVA-13? I mean she let her rage take possession of her so much that she didn't paid any attention to her battery level, and ended out of energy right in front of the enemy! And said enemy wasn't even trying to fight her! Asuka was really lucky that it was EVA-13 piloted by Shinji and not Kaworu or worst, the Mark.09, or she would had ended as paste.
